title: CIL Electrical — Advanced Mock 12 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the currency of the United Kingdom?
questions.solution: The UK's currency is the Pound Sterling (£).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where are the headquarters of the United Nations located?
questions.solution: The UN headquarters is located in New York City, USA.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where is the headquarters of the World Health Organization (WHO)?
questions.solution: The WHO is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is India's national space agency?
questions.solution: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is India's space agency.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Taj Mahal is located in which city?
questions.solution: The Taj Mahal is in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, on the bank of the Yamuna.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Sun Temple at Konark is located in which state?
questions.solution: The Konark Sun Temple is in Odisha, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In which year did the Dandi (Salt) March take place?
questions.solution: Gandhi led the Dandi Salt March in 1930 during the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Quit India Movement was launched in which year?
questions.solution: The Quit India Movement began in August 1942.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who founded the Maurya Empire?
questions.solution: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Maurya Empire around 321 BCE.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: How many players are there in a cricket team on the field?
questions.solution: A cricket team fields eleven players.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Thomas Cup is associated with which sport?
questions.solution: The Thomas Cup is the premier men's international team badminton event.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Bharat Ratna is India's highest:
questions.solution: The Bharat Ratna is the highest civilian award of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution regulates monetary policy in India?
questions.solution: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the country's central bank and monetary authority.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: GST in India was implemented from which date?
questions.solution: The Goods and Services Tax came into force on 1 July 2017.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately:
questions.solution: Light travels at about 3 × 10^8 metres per second in vacuum.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 4:36 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 4, M = 36.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times4-5.5\times36\right|=78^\circ$; smaller $=\min(78,360-78)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=78^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 10000 at 8% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 10000, r = 8% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=10000\left(\tfrac{8}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 64.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 221 km downstream in 13 h and the same 221 km upstream in 17 h. Its speed in still water is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed adds the stream; upstream subtracts it. Still-water speed is the average of the two.
**Formula.** $u=\tfrac12\left[(b+s)+(b-s)\right]$
**Given.** Downstream 221 km in 13 h; upstream 221 km in 17 h.
**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{221}{13}=17$ km/h, upstream $=\tfrac{221}{17}=13$ km/h.
**Answer.** Still-water speed $u=\tfrac{17+13}{2}=15$ km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills a tank in 5 h, but with a leak it takes 7 h. The leak alone can empty the full tank in:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The leak's rate is the fill-rate without the leak minus the slower fill-rate with the leak.
**Formula.** $T=\tfrac{ab}{b-a}$
**Given.** Fill without leak a = 5 h; with leak b = 7 h.
**Working.** Leak rate $=\tfrac1{5}-\tfrac1{7}$; emptying time $=\tfrac{5\times7}{7-5}$.
**Answer.** $T=17.5$ h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 120 m and 200 m long move in opposite directions at 72 km/h and 36 km/h. The time to cross each other is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite directions add speeds; convert km/h to m/s with the factor 5/18, then divide the combined length by the relative speed.
**Formula.** $t=\tfrac{L_1+L_2}{(s_1+s_2)\times\frac{5}{18}}$
**Given.** L₁ = 120 m, L₂ = 200 m, s₁ = 72 km/h, s₂ = 36 km/h.
**Working.** Relative $=(72+36)\times\tfrac{5}{18}=30$ m/s; $t=\tfrac{120+200}{30}$.
**Answer.** $t=10.67$ s.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 40% above cost, then gives a 10% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 40%, discount d = 10%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{40}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{10}{100}\right)=1.26$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=26\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 100 L of milk. 20 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 2 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 100 L, x = 20 L, n = 2.
**Working.** $=100\left(1-\tfrac{20}{100}\right)^{2}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 64$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 5000 for 12 months and B invests Rs 6000 for 4 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 3000, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 5000×12 mo; B: Rs 6000×4 mo; profit Rs 3000.
**Working.** Ratio $=60000:24000$; share$_A=3000\times\tfrac{60000}{84000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 2143.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 5:20 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 5, M = 20.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times5-5.5\times20\right|=40^\circ$; smaller $=\min(40,360-40)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=40^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The compound ratio of 4 : 3 and 5 : 9 is:
questions.solution: Compound ratio = (4×5) : (3×9) = 20 : 27.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An article sold at Rs 540 incurs a 10% loss. Its cost price is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 90 is divided in the ratio 3 : 7, the larger part is:
questions.solution: Larger part = 90 × 7/10 = 63.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of 12 and 15 is:
questions.solution: The lowest common multiple of 12 and 15 is 60.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A can do a work in 12 days and B in 30 days. Working together they finish it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Simple interest on Rs 6000 at 8% per annum for 4 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 7 gives quotient 13 and remainder 3. The number is:
questions.solution: Number = divisor × quotient + remainder = 7×13+3 = 94.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 10 men complete a work in 18 days, 9 men will complete it in:
questions.solution: Men-days constant: 10×18 = 9×D, D = 20 days.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The probability of getting an even number on a single throw of a fair die is:
questions.solution: Count the favourable outcomes out of 6 equally likely faces to get 1/2.

questions.stem: If 30% of a number is 90, the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'PEN' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: PEN → RGP.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 6 km towards north, then turns east and walks 8 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(6² + 8²) = √100 = 10 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: No A is B. All B are C. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution: Applying the rules of categorical syllogism, the valid conclusion is: Some C are not A.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'ACE'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of A, C, E to get 9.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 63 × 2 + 1 = 127.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'FISH' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: FISH → HKUJ.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: X is the husband of Y. Z is the mother of Y. How is Z related to X?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother-in-law.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 1 January is a Friday, what day is 8 January?
questions.solution: 8 − 1 = 7 days, exactly one week, so it is again Friday.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: M, N, O, P, ?
questions.solution: Consecutive alphabet letters; after P comes Q.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: Each number is halved, so 12 → 6.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 16, 25, 36, 49, ?
questions.solution: These are perfect squares; next = 8² = 64.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, STAR is coded as:
questions.solution: Shift each letter forward by one to get TUBS.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Head : Hat :: Foot : ?
questions.solution: Just as Hat relates to Head, Shoe relates to Foot.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 7, 21, 63, 189, ?
questions.solution: Each term is multiplied by 3, so next = 189 × 3 = 567.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 3, 6, 12, 21, ?
questions.solution: Differences grow by 3; adding the next difference gives 33.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing south turns 90° to the left. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning left from south leads to East.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 3, 7, 19, 25?
questions.solution: 3, 7, 19 are prime; 25 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: Comparing decimal values, 4/5 is the largest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A=1, B=2, ... then the sum of the letters of BED is:
questions.solution: Add the alphabet positions of B, E, D to get 11.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: GH, JK, MN, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after MN comes PQ.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All pens are tools. All tools are useful. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Both premises are universal affirmatives, so the chained conclusion 'All pens are useful' follows.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: The angle between the hands of a clock at 4:00 is:
questions.solution: Each hour = 30°; at 4:00 the hands are 4 × 30 = 120° apart.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 3 km north, then 4 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(3² + 4²) = 5 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 30 people, X is 7th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 30 − 7 + 1 = 24.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct meaning of the word 'Frugal'.
questions.solution: 'Frugal' means careful with money, i.e. economical.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Abundant'.
questions.solution: 'Abundant' means existing in large quantity, i.e. plentiful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Brief'.
questions.solution: 'Brief' means short or concise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Happy'.
questions.solution: 'Happy' is synonymous with 'joyful'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Enormous'.
questions.solution: 'Enormous' means very large, i.e. huge.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Diligent'.
questions.solution: 'Diligent' describes someone hard-working and careful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Candid'.
questions.solution: 'Candid' means open and frank in speech.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Vivid'.
questions.solution: 'Vivid' means bright, clear and intense.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Ancient'.
questions.solution: 'Ancient' (very old) is opposite to 'modern'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Victory'.
questions.solution: 'Victory' is opposite to 'defeat'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Expand'.
questions.solution: 'Expand' is opposite to 'contract'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Generous'.
questions.solution: 'Generous' is opposite to 'stingy' (mean with money).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Transparent'.
questions.solution: 'Transparent' (see-through) is opposite to 'opaque'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Optimist'.
questions.solution: An optimist's opposite is a 'pessimist'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.solution: The correct spelling is 'Occasion' (one c, two s).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She is good ___ mathematics.'
questions.solution: The idiom is 'good at' a subject or skill.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He has been living here ___ 2010.'
questions.solution: 'Since' is used with a point of time like a year.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Divide this ___ two parts.'
questions.solution: 'Divide into' is the correct collocation for separation.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is married ___ a doctor.'
questions.solution: The correct collocation is 'married to' someone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The book is ___ the table.'
questions.solution: 'On' indicates resting on a surface.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a person who cannot read or write':
questions.solution: Such a person is 'illiterate'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The study of living organisms is called:
questions.solution: 'Biology' is the study of all living organisms.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One who studies stars and celestial bodies is an:
questions.solution: An 'astronomer' scientifically studies celestial bodies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a speech made without preparation':
questions.solution: An unprepared speech is delivered 'extempore'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: A place where birds are kept is called:
questions.solution: An 'aviary' houses birds; an apiary keeps bees.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in an inductor carrying current I is:
questions.solution: Energy = ½LI², stored in the magnetic field of the coil.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SI unit of luminous flux is the:
questions.solution: Luminous flux is measured in lumens; illuminance (lumen/m²) is the lux.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which property does an ideal operational amplifier NOT have?
questions.solution: An ideal op-amp has infinite (not zero) open-loop gain, infinite input impedance and zero output impedance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC motor on 230 V draws 15 A with armature resistance 0.5 Ω. The back EMF is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As the slip of an induction motor increases beyond the value at maximum torque, the developed torque:
questions.solution: Torque rises with slip up to the breakdown point, then falls as slip increases further (the unstable region).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Adding a pole to the open-loop transfer function generally:
options: Reduces stability and makes the response more oscillatory
questions.solution: An extra pole adds phase lag, pushing the root locus toward the right half-plane and reducing stability margins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer with 200 primary and 400 secondary turns is fed 1100 V. The secondary voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'maximum demand' charge in a two-part tariff is based on:
options: The peak kVA/kW the consumer draws
questions.solution: A two-part tariff has a fixed charge tied to maximum demand plus an energy charge per kWh.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has L = 0.1 H and C = 10 µF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a first-order RC low-pass filter, which statement is correct?
options: It passes high frequencies and blocks low ones.
Above the cutoff frequency the gain rolls off at 20 dB/decade.
questions.solution: A first-order low-pass passes low frequencies; cutoff fc = 1/(2πRC) and roll-off is 20 dB/decade (single pole).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tellegen's theorem is based fundamentally on:
questions.solution: Tellegen's theorem holds for any lumped network satisfying KVL/KCL, regardless of element nature.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 50 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 50 Hz.)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The EMF equation of a transformer is E =
questions.solution: E = 4.44 f N Φm, from the rate of change of sinusoidal flux.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor has air-gap power 20 kW and slip 0.05. The rotor copper loss is:
questions.solution: Rotor copper loss = s × Pag = 0.05 × 20 kW = 1000 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Increasing the air gap in a magnetic circuit (other things equal):
questions.solution: The air gap adds large reluctance (low permeability), so for the same MMF the flux falls.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC series motor should never be started:
questions.solution: On no load a series motor races to dangerously high speed because flux falls with the small current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sinusoidal voltage shown has a peak of 100 V (marked). Its RMS value is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 120" width="300" height="120"><line x1="10" y1="60" x2="290" y2="60" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="10" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 60 Q 55 0 90 60 T 160 60 T 230 60 T 300 60" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="55" y1="60" x2="55" y2="10" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="60" y="14" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vm=100V</text><text x="270" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sinusoidal voltage waveform

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For an ideal buck (step-down) DC–DC converter in continuous conduction, the output voltage is:
questions.solution: A buck converter gives Vo = D·Vs; the boost converter gives Vs/(1−D).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sinusoidal voltage shown has a peak of 311 V (marked). Its RMS value is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 120" width="300" height="120"><line x1="10" y1="60" x2="290" y2="60" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="10" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 60 Q 55 0 90 60 T 160 60 T 230 60 T 300 60" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="55" y1="60" x2="55" y2="10" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="60" y="14" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vm=311V</text><text x="270" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sinusoidal voltage waveform

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a capacitor of capacitance C charged to voltage V is:
questions.solution: Energy = ½CV², stored in the electric field between the plates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At series resonance, the impedance of an RLC circuit is:
questions.solution: XL and XC cancel, leaving only R, so impedance is minimum and resistive (current maximum).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer delivers 10 kW with iron loss 0.2 kW and copper loss 0.6 kW. Its efficiency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superposition theorem is applicable only to:
questions.solution: Superposition holds for linear, bilateral networks; responses of independent sources add.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a load with R = 9 Ω and reactance X = 4 Ω, the power factor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Applying negative feedback to an amplifier, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: It stabilises the gain against parameter changes.
questions.solution: Negative feedback reduces gain (trading it for bandwidth, lower distortion and stability).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced 3-phase star system, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: Line voltage leads its nearest phase voltage by 30°.
The line current is √3 times the phase current.
Line and phase currents are equal.
The line voltage is √3 times the phase voltage.
questions.solution: In star, line and phase currents are equal; the √3 factor applies to voltages, not currents.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 50 Ω and S = 40 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×40/50 = 80 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=100Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=50Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=40Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transformers are rated in kVA rather than kW because:
options: Their losses depend on voltage and current, not on the load power factor
questions.solution: Iron loss depends on voltage and copper loss on current; both are independent of the load power factor, so apparent power (kVA) is the rating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 6-pole, 60 Hz induction motor runs at 1170 rpm. Its slip is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For maximum power transfer to a complex load ZL from a source of impedance Zth, ZL must equal:
questions.solution: With a reactive source, maximum power needs ZL = Zth* so the reactances cancel and resistances match.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The back EMF of a DC motor is:
questions.solution: Eb = (PφZN)/(60A); it rises with speed and flux and opposes the supply.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 12 V source drives a 6 Ω resistor. The current is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an induction motor, the rotor copper loss equals:
questions.solution: Of the air-gap power Pag, a fraction s is dissipated as rotor copper loss and (1 − s) becomes mechanical power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series RLC circuit has R = 10 Ω, L = 0.2 H, C = 10 µF. Its quality factor Q is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a purely capacitive AC circuit, the current:
options: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.solution: An ideal capacitor makes current lead voltage by 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The type of a control system is the number of:
options: Poles at the origin of the open-loop transfer function
questions.solution: System 'type' = number of integrators (s-factors) in the denominator at s = 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has R = 10 Ω, XL = 12 Ω, XC = 4 Ω across 100 V. The current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The V-curve of a synchronous motor is a plot of:
options: Armature current versus field current at constant load
questions.solution: Varying field excitation at fixed load traces a V-shaped armature-current curve, minimum at unity pf.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 6 Ω and X = 3 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=6Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=3Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>
figure.caption: Impedance triangle (R–X)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time constant of a series RC circuit is:
questions.solution: τ = RC; after one time constant the capacitor charges to ~63% of the final voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer operates on the principle of:
questions.solution: Changing flux from the primary links the secondary, inducing an EMF (mutual induction).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of a 4-pole machine on a 60 Hz supply is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most widely used semiconductor material for power devices is:
questions.solution: Silicon dominates because of its stable oxide, temperature tolerance and cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 48 V source drives a 4 Ω resistor. The current is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) is a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: KCL says the algebraic sum of currents at a node is zero — charge is neither created nor destroyed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The main purpose of earthing the metal body of an appliance is:
options: Safety of personnel against electric shock
questions.solution: Earthing gives fault current a low-resistance path and keeps exposed metal near earth potential, protecting users.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.5 H, C = 40 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.5×40µF)) ≈ 35.6 Hz.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" width="300" height="90"><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="40" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="40" y="37" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="57" y="32" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">R</text><path d="M74 45 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="98" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">L=0.5H</text><line x1="122" y1="45" x2="150" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="35" x2="150" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="158" y1="35" x2="158" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="154" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">C=40µF</text><line x1="158" y1="45" x2="290" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="290" y1="45" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="10" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="75" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="75" r="3" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="88" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">~ AC source</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series RLC circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Under maximum power transfer conditions, the efficiency of power transfer is:
questions.solution: With RL = Rth, equal power is lost in Rth and delivered to RL, so efficiency is only 50%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coil has resistance 6 Ω and reactance 6 Ω. Its impedance magnitude is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The resonant frequency of a series RLC circuit is given by:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, giving f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about ideal sources is INCORRECT?
options: An ideal voltage source maintains its voltage for any load current.
An ideal voltage source has zero internal resistance.
An ideal current source has zero internal resistance.
An ideal current source has infinite internal resistance.
questions.solution: An ideal current source has infinite internal resistance; zero internal resistance describes an ideal voltage source.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current of 4 A flows through a 8 Ω resistor. The power dissipated is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A step-up transformer has:
options: More secondary turns than primary turns
questions.solution: Step-up means N₂ > N₁, so V₂ > V₁.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a 470 µF capacitor charged to 10 V is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor develops maximum torque when the rotor resistance per phase equals:
options: The standstill rotor reactance times the slip (R₂ = sX₂)
questions.solution: Maximum torque occurs at the slip where R₂ = sX₂; the peak torque itself is independent of R₂.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ohm's law states that, at constant temperature, the current through a conductor is:
options: Directly proportional to the voltage across it
Proportional to the square of the voltage
questions.solution: V = IR, so for constant R the current rises in direct proportion to the applied voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (12 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 150" width="220" height="150"><line x1="30" y1="20" x2="30" y2="50" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="50" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">R1=2Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="84" x2="30" y2="100" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="100" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R2=4Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="134" x2="30" y2="145" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="30" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="40" y="96" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text><text x="35" y="16" font-size="10" fill="#475569">12V</text></svg>
figure.caption: Resistive voltage divider

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The equivalent resistance of two equal resistors R in parallel is:
questions.solution: For two equal parallel resistors, Req = R·R/(R+R) = R/2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 6 Ω and X = 6 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=6Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=6Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>
figure.caption: Impedance triangle (R–X)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The EMF of a single lead-acid cell is about:
questions.solution: A lead-acid cell gives ~2 V; a 12 V battery uses six cells in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical power dissipated in a resistor can be written as:
questions.solution: P = VI = I²R = V²/R for a resistor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 3 Ω and 12 Ω are connected in series. The equivalent resistance is:
questions.solution: Series adds: 3 + 12 = 15 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A thyristor (SCR) is turned ON by:
options: A gate pulse while forward biased
questions.solution: A gate current triggers the SCR into conduction when the anode is positive; it then latches.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has R = 8 Ω, XL = 20 Ω, XC = 12 Ω across 240 V. The current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The form factor of a sinusoidal waveform is:
questions.solution: Form factor = RMS/average (half cycle) = 0.707/0.637 ≈ 1.11.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 4 Ω and 6 Ω in series give a total resistance of:
questions.solution: Series resistances simply add: 4 + 6 = 10 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series RLC circuit has R = 10 Ω, L = 0.2 H, C = 100 µF. Its quality factor Q is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Norton's theorem replaces a linear network by:
options: A current source in parallel with a resistance
A voltage source in series with a resistance
questions.solution: Norton: a current source IN in parallel with RN (= Rth).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinusoidal voltage has a peak value of 141 V. Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a single line-to-ground fault, the three sequence networks are connected in:
questions.solution: An LG fault connects positive, negative and zero sequence networks in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time constant of a series RL circuit is:
questions.solution: τ = L/R; the current reaches ~63% of its final value in one time constant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 6 Ω and 12 Ω are connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a reciprocal two-port described by ABCD parameters, the condition satisfied is:
questions.solution: Reciprocity of a passive two-port gives the determinant AD − BC = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A copper wire (ρ = 1.7×10⁻⁸ Ω·m) is 100 m long with 1 mm² cross-section. Its resistance is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Ferranti effect on a long, lightly loaded transmission line means:
questions.solution: Line capacitance draws leading current that raises the receiving-end voltage above the sending end under light load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a purely inductive AC circuit, the current:
options: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.solution: An ideal inductor makes current lag voltage by 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The capacitive reactance of a 200 µF capacitor at 50 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A diode conducts when it is:
questions.solution: A diode conducts in forward bias and blocks in reverse bias (ideally).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.5 H, C = 100 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.5×100µF)) ≈ 22.5 Hz.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" width="300" height="90"><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="40" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="40" y="37" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="57" y="32" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">R</text><path d="M74 45 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="98" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">L=0.5H</text><line x1="122" y1="45" x2="150" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="35" x2="150" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="158" y1="35" x2="158" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="154" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">C=100µF</text><line x1="158" y1="45" x2="290" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="290" y1="45" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="10" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="75" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="75" r="3" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="88" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">~ AC source</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series RLC circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The RMS value of a sinusoidal voltage of peak value Vm is:
questions.solution: For a sine wave, RMS = Vm/√2 ≈ 0.707 Vm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The efficiency of a transformer is maximum when:
questions.solution: Maximum efficiency occurs at the load where variable copper loss = constant iron loss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The inductive reactance of a 0.02 H inductor at 50 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL) is a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: The sum of voltage drops around a closed loop equals zero — energy supplied equals energy absorbed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of a 4-pole machine on a 50 Hz supply is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Regarding a synchronous motor, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: Over-excitation makes it draw leading current.
It runs at exactly synchronous speed in steady state.
It can be used to improve system power factor.
It is inherently self-starting from rest.
questions.solution: A synchronous motor produces no net starting torque at standstill, so it is not self-starting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For maximum power transfer to a resistive load from a DC source, the load resistance should equal:
questions.solution: Maximum power is transferred when RL = Rth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a divider, 12 V is applied across 6 Ω and 4 Ω in series. The voltage across the 4 Ω resistor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Routh–Hurwitz criterion is used to determine:
options: The number of closed-loop poles in the right half-plane
questions.solution: Sign changes in the first column of the Routh array count the RHP poles (unstable roots).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 20 A at 400 V line, pf 0.8. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The holding current of a thyristor is:
options: The minimum anode current to keep it conducting after the gate is removed
The current needed to latch it on
questions.solution: Holding current keeps an already-on SCR conducting; latching current (larger) is needed to turn it on.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average value of a sinusoidal current over one complete cycle is:
questions.solution: Over a full cycle the positive and negative halves cancel, giving zero average.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 20 Ω, Q = 10 Ω and S = 60 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 20×60/10 = 120 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=20Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=10Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=60Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thevenin's theorem replaces a linear network by:
options: A current source in parallel with a resistance
A voltage source in series with a resistance
questions.solution: Thevenin: an equivalent EMF Vth in series with Rth seen from the terminals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A source has Vth = 40 V and Rth = 5 Ω. The maximum power deliverable to a matched load is:
questions.solution: Pmax = Vth²/(4Rth) = 40²/(4×5) = 80 W (load = Rth, efficiency 50%).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative feedback in an amplifier generally:
options: Reduces gain but increases bandwidth and stability
questions.solution: Negative feedback trades gain for stability, lower distortion and wider bandwidth (gain–bandwidth product is roughly constant).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power factor is defined as the cosine of the angle between:
questions.solution: Power factor = cos φ, where φ is the phase angle between voltage and current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 60 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 60 Hz.)

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" height="130"><line x1="20" y1="110" x2="290" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="15" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 108 C 110 105 140 20 160 20 C 180 20 210 105 290 108" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="160" y1="20" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="160" y="124" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f₀</text><text x="26" y="20" font-size="10" fill="#475569">I</text><text x="278" y="124" font-size="10" fill="#475569">f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series-resonance current curve

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current transformer (CT) secondary should never be:
questions.solution: An open CT secondary produces a dangerously high voltage and core saturation; it must be shorted if the meter is removed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 1000 W and W₂ = 400 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 1000 + 400 = 1400 W (the difference relates to reactive power).
